summary: Iteration 3 regression for Free/Premium plans + Super Admin. Extended backend_test.py with 14 new tests (Plans, FreePlanLimits, SuperAdminAuthGate, SuperAdminFlows). Total 33/33 pytest pass. Playwright verified Landing #pricing cards, /admin redirect when unauthenticated, super@go-next.in login landing on /admin with stats/tabs/upgrade buttons, premium owner header plan-badge='PREMIUM', fresh free-user Outlets page showing plan-badge='FREE PLAN' + upgrade-banner + disabled new-outlet-btn, and admin guard redirecting free owner away from /admin to their dashboard.
action_items: (Nice-to-have) Seed super-admin password should be updated idempotently if ADMIN_PASSWORD env changes (same comment as iter 2).
(Nice-to-have) /api/auth/login has no brute-force lockout after 5 failed attempts — playbook item still open.
(Nice-to-have) /api/plans could be cached (Cache-Control: public, max-age=300) since it's static.
(Nice-to-have) In /api/admin/users the super_admin users are filtered out (role:'owner' only) — consider surfacing super_admins too in a separate section for visibility.
critical_code_review_comments: create_business_doc forces token_limit=min(50, plan_max) on every create even for premium — so a new premium outlet starts at 50/day until the owner explicitly raises it. Intentional per agent note, but worth documenting in UI.

PATCH /api/business/{id} plan check uses user's current plan; if super admin upgrades a user's plan mid-session, the owner's cookie-based JWT still authenticates and the DB lookup in get_current_user correctly reflects the new plan (confirmed by test_upgrade_free_to_premium_unlocks_second_outlet). Good.
AdminUserUpdate only accepts {plan} but not {role}; super-admins cannot demote/promote role via this endpoint — may be intentional but worth noting.
admin_update_user does `updated.pop('password_hash', None)` after find_one with projection already excluding password_hash — redundant but harmless.
Plan limits hard-coded in PLAN_LIMITS dict; if pricing ever needs to change, a settings table is more flexible.
test_credentials: demo@go-next.in / admin123 (premium owner), super@go-next.in / admin123 (super admin). Fresh free users registered per test with test_free_* emails.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_*-prefixed outlets and walk-ins; Playwright creates one ui_free_*@demo.com user with one outlet UI_FreeOutlet. These linger in the DB (harmless, prefixed for easy identification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py now covers: TestPlans (public /api/plans), TestFreePlanLimits (register->free, 2nd-outlet 403, chairs>2 403, token_limit>50 403, premium admin multi-outlet), TestSuperAdminAuthGate (401/403), TestSuperAdminFlows (stats shape, users+outlet_count, businesses+owner_email, upgrade unlocks 2nd outlet, delete+cascade). fresh_free_owner fixture registers unique email each time so tests are idempotent. Frontend data-testids verified: pricing-free, pricing-premium, plan-badge, upgrade-banner, new-outlet-btn, login-email, login-password, login-submit. Admin tabs are selected by text (Owners/Outlets/Overview) — main agent could add data-testids for admin-stat-* cards and admin-tab-* for more robust future tests.
rca of the issue: N/A - no regressions. All plan-enforcement, admin-guard, and super-admin features behave as specified.
